mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production reflects expanding supply-side capacity utilization and corporate revenue momentum, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following month-over-month INDPRO increases are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate signals labor market deterioration that reduces household income expectations and corporate earnings forecasts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE rises >0.3pp over 3 months during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rising consumer prices and a stagnant or falling nominal federal funds rate compress real interest rates, stimulating corporate borrowing and equity valuations, which produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases month-over-month while FEDFUNDS is unchanged or decreases
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with inflationary pressure
failure_condition: In months where CPIAUCSL rises and FEDFUNDS is flat or down, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 45% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market, rising retail sales confirm robust consumer demand that is less sensitive to job loss fears, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 5.0% and RSAFS rises month-over-month, the average 5-day S&P 500 forward return is not significantly greater than zero (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth above 2.5% annualized signals demand running ahead of potential, lifting corporate revenue growth and profit margins, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Following quarters with GDPC1 growth > 2.5%, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return <= 0% or t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Back-to-back monthly declines in industrial production indicate a contraction in goods-producing sectors that drags down aggregate earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: After two consecutive monthly declines in INDPRO, S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of walk-forward instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payrolls alongside falling industrial production signal labor hoarding or divergence toward services, which squeezes productivity and corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month while INDPRO decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: Joint occurrences of month-over-month PAYEMS increases and INDPRO decreases are followed by positive S&P 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A material drop in housing starts signals weakening residential investment and downstream demand for construction materials and consumer durables, which foreshadows negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST declines by more than 5% over 3 months, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.
